Subparagraph.Text: to an official of a department or agency of the Government of Canada or of a province as to the name, address, telephone number, occupation, size or type of business of a person, solely for the purposes of enabling that department or agency to obtain statistical data for research and analysis,

Subsection.Text: Subsection 295(5) of the Act is amended by striking out the word “or” at the end of paragraph (k), by adding the word “or” at the end of paragraph (l) and by adding the following after paragraph (l):

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: provide confidential information to any person solely for the purpose of enabling the Chief Statistician, within the meaning assigned by section 2 of the Statistics Act, to provide to a statistical agency of a province data concerning business activities carried on in the province, if the information is used by the statistical agency of the province solely for research and analysis and that statistical agency is authorized under the law of the province to collect the same or similar information on its own behalf in respect of such activities.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 295(5) of the Act is amended by striking out the word “or” at the end of paragraph (l), by adding the word “or” at the end of paragraph (m), as enacted by subsection (2), and by adding the following after paragraph (m):

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: provide confidential information, or allow the inspection of or access to confidential information, as the case may be, solely for the purposes of a provision contained in a listed international agreement.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (2) applies after this Act receives royal assent to information relating to fiscal years that end after 2003 and, for the purpose of subsection 17(2) of the Statistics Act, if the information was collected before this Act receives royal assent, the information is deemed to have been collected at the time at which it is provided to a statistical agency of a province pursuant to paragraph 295(5)(m) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (2).

Section.Text: Subsection 303(4) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Le ministre peut recevoir la demande qui n’a pas été livrée ou postée à la personne ou à l’endroit indiqué au paragraphe (3).

Section.Text: Subsection 308(2) of the Act is repealed.
Paragraph 328(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to whom confidential information has been provided for a particular purpose under paragraph 295(5)(b), (c), (g), (k), (l), (m) or (n), or

Subsection.Text: The portion of the definition practitioner in section 1 of Part II of Schedule V to Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: practitioner, in respect of a supply of optometric, chiropractic, physiotherapy, chiropodic, podiatric, osteopathic, audiological, speech-language pathology, occupational therapy, psychological or dietetic services, means a person who

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: practises the profession of optometry, chiropractic, physiotherapy, chiropody, podiatry, osteopathy, audiology, speech-language pathology, occupational therapy, psychology or dietetics, as the case may be,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (c)(i) of the definition établissement de santé in section 1 of Part II of Schedule V to the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: des soins infirmiers et personnels sous la direction ou la surveillance d’un personnel de soins infirmiers et médicaux compétent ou d’autres soins personnels et de surveillance (sauf les services ménagers propres à la tenue de l’intérieur domestique) selon les besoins des résidents,

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (c) of the definition établissement de santé in section 1 of Part II of Schedule V to the French version of the Act is amended by adding the following after subparagraph (ii):

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: les repas et le logement.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to any supply made after 2000.
Subsections (2) and (3) are deemed to have come into force on December 17, 1990.
Paragraph 7(h) of Part II of Schedule V to the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: speech-language pathology services;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to supplies made after 2000.
Part II of Schedule V to the Act is amended by adding the following after section 7.1:

Section.Text: A supply of a service rendered in the practise of the profession of social work where

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the service is rendered to an individual within a professional-client relationship between the supplier and the individual and is provided for the prevention, assessment or remediation of, or to assist the individual in coping with, a physical, emotional, behavioural or mental disorder or disability of the individual or of another person to whom the individual is related or to whom the individual provides care or supervision otherwise than in a professional capacity; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: either

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the supplier is required to be licensed or otherwise certified to practise the profession of social work in the province in which the service is supplied, the supplier is so licensed or certified, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the supplier is not required to be licensed or otherwise certified to practise that profession in that province, the supplier has the qualifications equivalent to those necessary to be licensed or certified to practise that profession in a province in which such a requirement exists.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to any supply made after October 3, 2003.

If a person is, or would be in the absence of subsection 261(3) of the Act, entitled to a rebate under section 261 of the Act in respect of an amount that was paid before the day on which this Act receives royal assent (in this subsection and subsection (4) referred to as the “assent date”) as tax in respect of a supply described in section 7.2 of Part II of Schedule V to the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), but not described in any other section of any Part of that Schedule, the person may, despite subsection 261(3) of the Act, file an application for the rebate before the later of the day that is one year after the assent date and the day that is two years after the day on which the amount was paid.

If a person is, or would be in the absence of the two-year period referred to in subsection 232(1) of the Act, entitled to adjust, refund or credit, in accordance with section 232 of the Act, an amount that was charged or collected before the assent date as or on account of tax in respect of a supply described in section 7.2 of Part II of Schedule V to the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), but not described in any other section of any Part of that Schedule, the person may, despite the two-year period referred to in subsection 232(1) of the Act, adjust the amount under paragraph 232(1)(a) of the Act, or refund or credit the amount under paragraph 232(1)(b) of the Act, before the later of the day that is one year after the assent date and the day that is two years after the day on which the amount was charged or collected.
Paragraph 1(d) of Part V.1 of Schedule V to the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: tangible personal property (other than property supplied by way of lease, licence or similar arrangement in conjunction with an exempt supply by the charity of real property by way of lease, licence or similar arrangement) that was acquired, manufactured or produced by the charity for the purpose of making a supply of the property and was neither donated to the charity nor used by another person before its acquisition by the charity, or any service supplied by the charity in respect of such property, other than such property or such a service supplied by the charity under a contract for catering;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to any supply for which consideration becomes due after 1996 or is paid after 1996 without having become due, but does not apply to a supply in respect of which tax under Part IX of the Act was charged or collected on or before October 3, 2003.

For the purposes of Part IX of the Act, if, because of the application of paragraph 1(d) of Part V.1 of Schedule V to the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), a charity is considered to have ceased at any time to use capital property of the charity primarily in commercial activities of the charity and is deemed under subsection 200(2) of the Act to have made, immediately before that time, a supply of the property and to have collected tax in respect of that supply, and that cessation would not be considered to have occurred at that time if subsection (1) were not enacted,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the charity is not required to include that tax in determining its net tax for any reporting period; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of determining the basic tax content (as defined in subsection 123(1) of the Act) of the property, the charity is deemed to have been entitled to recover an amount equal to that tax as a rebate of tax included in the description of A in that definition.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 20(a) to (e) of Part VI of Schedule V to the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a supply of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a service of registering, or processing an application to register, any property in a property registration system,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a service of filing, or processing an application to file, any document in a property registration system, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a right to have access to, or to use, a property registration system to register, or make application to register, any property in it or to file, or make application to file, any document in it,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a supply of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a service of filing, or processing an application to file, a document in the registration system of a court or in accordance with legislative requirements,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a right to have access to, or to use, the registration system of a court, or any other registration system in which documents are filed in accordance with legislative requirements, for the purpose of filing a document in that registration system,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a service of issuing or providing, or processing an application to issue or provide, a document from the registration system of a court or other similar tribunal, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a right to have access to, or to use, the registration system of a court or other similar tribunal to issue or obtain a document from that registration system,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a supply (other than of a right or service supplied in respect of the importation of alcoholic beverages) of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a licence, permit, quota or similar right,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a service of processing an application for a licence, permit, quota or similar right, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a right to have access to, or to use, a filing or registration system to make application for a licence, permit, quota or similar right,

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a supply of any document, a service of providing information, or a right to have access to, or to use, a filing or registration system to obtain any document or information that indicates

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the vital statistics, residency, citizenship or right to vote of any person,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the registration of any person for any service provided by a government or municipality or by a board, commission or other body established by a government or municipality, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: any other status of any person,

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a supply of any document, a service of providing information, or a right to have access to, or to use, a filing or registration system to obtain any document or information, in respect of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the title to, or any right or estate in, property,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any encumbrance or assessment in respect of property, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the zoning of real property,

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 20(l) of Part VI of Schedule V to the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: a supply of a right to enter, to have access to or to use property of the government, municipality or other body other than a right, referred to in any of paragraphs (a) to (e), to have access to, or to use, a filing or registration system.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on December 17, 1990, except that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: paragraphs 20(a), (b), (d) and (e) of Part VI of Schedule V to the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), and paragraph 20(l) of that Part, as enacted by subsection (2), do not apply to any supply in respect of which the supplier, on or before November 27, 2006, charged or collected an amount as or on account of tax under Part IX of the Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 20(c) of Part VI of Schedule V to the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), does not apply to any supply

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: that is a supply of a right to have access to, or to use, a filing or registration system in respect of which the supplier, on or before November 27, 2006, charged or collected an amount as or on account of tax under Part IX of the Act, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: that is a supply of a service, made on or before November 27, 2006,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: in respect of which the supplier did not, on or before that day, charge or collect any amount as or on account of tax under Part IX of the Act, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: in respect of which the supplier charged or collected, on or before that day, an amount as or on account of tax and in respect of which supply an amount (other than an amount deemed under paragraph 296(5)(a) of the Act to have been claimed as a result of an assessment made after that day) was claimed

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: in an application under subsection 261(1) of the Act received by the Minister of National Revenue on or before that day, or

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: as a deduction, in respect of any adjustment, refund or credit under subsection 232(1) of the Act, in a return under Division V of Part IX of the Act received by the Minister before that day; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in relation to supplies for which consideration becomes due before 1997, or is paid before 1997 without having become due, paragraph 20(e) of Part VI of Schedule V to the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), shall be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a supply of any document, a service of providing information, or a right to have access to, or to use, a filing or registration system to obtain any document or information, in respect of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the title to, or any right or estate in, property, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any encumbrance in respect of property,

Subsection.Text: The portion of section 2 of Part I of Schedule VI to the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: A supply of any of the following drugs or substances:

Subsection.Text: Section 2 of Part I of Schedule VI to the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (d.1)
Paragraph.Text: a drug included in Schedule 1 to the Benzodiazepines and Other Targeted Substances Regulations,

Subsection.Text: Section 2 of Part I of Schedule VI to the Act is amended by striking out the word “and” at the end of paragraph (e), by adding the word “and” at the end of paragraph (f) and by replacing the portion after paragraph (f) with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: plasma expander,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: but not including a supply of a drug or substance when it is labelled or supplied for agricultural or veterinary use only.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (3) apply to any supply made after April 12, 2001 and to any supply for which consideration becomes due after that day or is paid after that day without having become due.
Subsection (2) is deemed to have come into force on September 1, 2000 except that it does not apply

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to any supply made after August 2000 and on or before November 27, 2006 if, on or before that day, the supplier collected any amount as or on account of tax under Part IX of the Act in respect of the supply;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of section 6 of Schedule VII to the Act, to drugs imported after August 2000 and on or before November 27, 2006 if, on or before that day, any amount was paid as or on account of tax under Part IX of the Act in respect of the importation; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of section 15 of Part I of Schedule X to the Act, to drugs brought into a participating province after August 2000 and on or before November 27, 2006 if, on or before that day, any amount was paid as or on account of tax under Part IX of the Act in respect of the bringing into the province.

Subsection.Text: Part IV of Schedule VI to the Act is amended by adding the following after section 3:

Section.Text: A supply of grain or seeds, or of mature stalks having no leaves, flowers, seeds or branches, of hemp plants of the genera Cannabis, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of grain or seeds, they are not further processed than sterilized or treated for seeding purposes and are not packaged, prepared or sold for use as feed for wild birds or as pet food;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of viable grain or seeds, they are included in the definition industrial hemp in section 1 of the Industrial Hemp Regulations made under the Controlled Drugs and Substances Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the supply is made in accordance with that Act, if applicable.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to any supply for which consideration becomes due after April 12, 2001 or is paid after that day without having become due.
Section 6 of Schedule VII to the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Goods the supply of which is included in any of Parts I to IV and VIII of Schedule VI, other than section 3.1 of Part IV of that Schedule.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to goods imported after April 12, 2001.
Section 8.3 of Schedule VII to the Act is repealed.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on November 17, 2005.
Schedule VII to the Act is amended by adding the following after section 11:

Section.Text: Imported grain or seeds, or imported mature stalks having no leaves, flowers, seeds or branches, of hemp plants of the genera Cannabis, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of grain or seeds, they are not further processed than sterilized or treated for seeding purposes and are not packaged, prepared or sold for use as feed for wild birds or as pet food;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of viable grain or seeds, they are included in the definition industrial hemp in section 1 of the Industrial Hemp Regulations made under the Controlled Drugs and Substances Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the importation is in accordance with that Act, if applicable.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to grain or seeds and mature stalks imported after April 12, 2001.
Section 22 of Part I of Schedule X to the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Property (other than a specified motor vehicle) that is brought into a participating province by a registrant (other than a registrant whose net tax is determined under section 225.1 of the Act or under Part IV or V of the Streamlined Accounting (GST/HST) Regulations) for consumption, use or supply exclusively in the course of commercial activities of the registrant.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to property brought into a participating province after April 2002.
The Act is amended by replacing “(GST)” with “(GST/HST)” in the following provisions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 195.2(1)(b);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 195.2(2);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 220.07(2)(a);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 225.1(10);

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 227(4.2);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 227(6); and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: section 1 of Part II of Schedule X.

Subsection.Text: The English version of the Act is amended by replacing “(GST)” with “(GST/HST)” in the following provisions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 352(9)(c);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subparagraph 352(10)(c)(i); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: subparagraph 354(2)(c)(i).

Subsection.Text: The French version of the Act is amended by replacing “(TPS)” with the term “(TPS/TVH)” in the following provisions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 352(9)(a);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subparagraph 352(10)(a)(i); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: subparagraph 354(2)(a)(i).

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (3) are deemed to have come into force on April 1, 1997.

Heading.TitleText: Amendments in Respect of Excise Taxes

Section.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the Excise Tax Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: listed international agreement means the Convention on Mutual Administrative Assistance in Tax Matters, concluded at Strasbourg on January 25, 1988, as amended from time to time.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 88(1) of the Act, as enacted by subsection 134(1) of the Budget Implementation Act, 2006, chapter 4 of the Statutes of Canada, 2006, is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Waiver or cancellation of interest or penalty

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, on or before the day that is 10 calendar years after the end of a reporting period of a person, or on application by the person on or before that day, waive or cancel any amount otherwise payable to the Receiver General under this Act that is interest or a penalty on an amount that is required to be paid by the person under this Act in respect of the reporting period.
Subsection (1) comes into force, or is deemed to have come into force, on April 1, 2007.

Section.Text: Subsection 99(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Provision of documents may be required

Subsection.Text: Subject to section 102.1, the Minister may, for any purpose related to the administration or enforcement of this Act, or of a listed international agreement, by a notice served personally or by registered or certified mail, require that any person provide any book, record, writing or other document or any information or further information within any reasonable time that may be stipulated in the notice.

Heading.Label: PART 2
Heading.TitleText: AMENDMENTS IN RESPECT OF EXCISE DUTY ON ALCOHOL AND TOBACCO PRODUCTS

Subsection.Text: The definition tobacco dealer in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001 is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: tobacco dealer means a person, other than a tobacco licensee, who purchases for resale, sells or offers to sell raw leaf tobacco on which duty is not imposed under this Act.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs (f) and (g) of the definition spirits in section 2 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: fusel oil or other refuse produced as a result of the distillation process;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: an approved formulation; or

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: any product containing or manufactured from a material or substance referred to in paragraphs (b) to (g) that is not consumable as a beverage.

Subsection.Text: The portion of the definition marquer in section 2 of the French version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: marquer Apposer, en la forme et selon les modalités prévues par règlement, une mention portant :

Subsection.Text: Section 2 of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: listed international agreement means the Convention on Mutual Administrative Assistance in Tax Matters, concluded at Strasbourg on January 25, 1988, as amended from time to time.

restricted formulation means an approved formulation on which the Minister has imposed the condition or restriction under section 143 that the formulation can only be used by a licensed user or be exported.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (3) and the definition restricted formulation in section 2 of the Act, as enacted by subsection (4), are deemed to have come into force on April 1, 2003.
Paragraph 14(1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a user’s licence, authorizing the person to use bulk alcohol, non-duty-paid packaged alcohol or a restricted formulation;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 14(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:
A person is not entitled to a licence under paragraph (1)(a) by reason only of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: having been deemed to have produced spirits under section 131.2; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: having produced spirits for the purpose or as a consequence of the analysis of the composition of a substance containing absolute ethyl alcohol.

Subsection.Text: Subject to the regulations, on application by a person who is the holder of a spirits licence and a user’s licence, the Minister may issue to the person a wine licence, authorizing the person to fortify wine.
Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on April 1, 2003.
Section 17 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Subject to the regulations, on application, the Minister may issue an alcohol registration to a person authorizing the person to store or transport bulk alcohol, specially denatured alcohol or a restricted formulation.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on April 1, 2003.
Subsection 19(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to the regulations, on application, the Minister may issue an excise warehouse licence to a person who is not a retailer of alcohol authorizing the person to possess in their excise warehouse non-duty-paid packaged alcohol or manufactured tobacco or cigars that are not stamped.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on April 1, 2003.
Subsection 20(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to the regulations, on application, the Minister may issue a special excise warehouse licence to a person who is authorized by a tobacco licensee to be the only person, other than the licensee, who is entitled to distribute to an accredited representative manufactured tobacco or cigars manufactured by the licensee.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on April 1, 2003.
Section 21 of the Act is replaced by the following:
If a person ceases to be authorized by a tobacco licensee to distribute to an accredited representative manufactured tobacco or cigars manufactured by the tobacco licensee,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person shall immediately return the tobacco or cigars of that licensee that are stored in the person’s special excise warehouse to the excise warehouse of the tobacco licensee; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the tobacco licensee shall immediately notify the Minister in writing that the person has ceased to be so authorized.

Subsection.Text: The Minister shall cancel the special excise warehouse licence of the person if the person is no longer authorized by any tobacco licensee to distribute to an accredited representative manufactured tobacco or cigars.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on April 1, 2003.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 24:

Section.MarginalNote: Licences and registrations not statutory instruments
Section.Text: For greater certainty, a licence or registration issued under this Act is not a statutory instrument for the purposes of the Statutory Instruments Act.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on April 1, 2003.
Subsection 25(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:
An individual who is not a tobacco licensee may manufacture manufactured tobacco or cigars

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: from packaged raw leaf tobacco or manufactured tobacco on which the duty has been paid, if the tobacco or cigars are for their personal use; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: from raw leaf tobacco grown on land on which the individual resides, if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the tobacco or cigars are for their personal use or that of the members of their family who reside with the individual and who are 18 years of age or older, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the quantity of tobacco or cigars manufactured in any year does not exceed 15 kg for the individual and each member of the individual’s family who resides with the individual and who is 18 years of age or older.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Paragraph 28(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: raw leaf tobacco for

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: return to a licensed tobacco dealer or a tobacco grower,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: delivery to another tobacco licensee, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: export; or

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 28:

Section.MarginalNote: Unlawful removal from premises of tobacco dealer

Subsection.Text: No person shall remove raw leaf tobacco from the premises of a licensed tobacco dealer.
Subsection (1) does not apply to a licensed tobacco dealer who removes from their premises raw leaf tobacco for

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: return to a tobacco grower;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: delivery to a tobacco licensee or to another licensed tobacco dealer; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: export.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Paragraphs 30(2)(a) to (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a person who is a tobacco licensee or a licensed tobacco dealer; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the possession of raw leaf tobacco

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in a customs bonded warehouse or a sufferance warehouse by the licensee of that warehouse,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: by a body established under provincial law for the marketing of raw leaf tobacco grown in the province, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: by a prescribed person who is transporting the tobacco under prescribed circumstances and conditions.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Subparagraph 31(a)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: for delivery to or return from a tobacco licensee or a licensed tobacco dealer, or

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Paragraph 32(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a tobacco licensee at the place of manufacture of the product;

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of manufactured tobacco or cigars, a tobacco licensee who manufactured the tobacco or cigars, at their excise warehouse;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 32(2)(c) to (e) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of manufactured tobacco or cigars, a special excise warehouse licensee at the special excise warehouse of the licensee, if the licensee is permitted under this Act to distribute the tobacco or cigars;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an imported tobacco product, a prescribed person who is transporting the product under prescribed circumstances and conditions;

Paragraph.Label: (d.1)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of manufactured tobacco or cigars manufactured in Canada, a prescribed person who is transporting the tobacco or cigars under prescribed circumstances and conditions;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an imported tobacco product, a sufferance warehouse licensee in their sufferance warehouse;

Paragraph.Label: (e.1)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of imported manufactured tobacco or cigars, a customs bonded warehouse licensee in their customs bonded warehouse;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 32(2)(h) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of manufactured tobacco or cigars, an accredited representative for their personal or official use;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 32(2)(k) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of manufactured tobacco or cigars, an individual who has manufactured the tobacco or cigars in accordance with subsection 25(3).

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 32(3)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a tobacco licensee sells or offers to sell manufactured tobacco or cigars that are exported by the licensee in accordance with this Act;

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs 32(3)(b)(i) and (ii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: manufactured tobacco or cigars to a special excise warehouse licensee, if the special excise warehouse licensee is permitted under this Act to distribute the tobacco or cigars,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: manufactured tobacco or cigars to an accredited representative for their personal or official use,

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 32(3)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a special excise warehouse licensee sells or offers to sell manufactured tobacco or cigars to an accredited representative for their personal or official use, if the licensee is permitted under this Act to distribute the tobacco or cigars;

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs 32(3)(d)(i) and (ii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: imported manufactured tobacco or cigars that are exported by the licensee in accordance with this Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: imported manufactured tobacco or cigars to an accredited representative for their personal or official use or to a duty free shop, or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 32(3)(g) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a customs bonded warehouse licensee sells or offers to sell imported manufactured tobacco or cigars that are exported by the licensee in accordance with this Act;

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 32(3)(h) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: a customs bonded warehouse licensee sells or offers to sell imported manufactured tobacco or cigars

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (10) are deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Paragraph 35(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: manufactured tobacco or cigars that a tobacco licensee is authorized to import under subsection 41(2);

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 35(2)(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: raw leaf tobacco that is imported by a tobacco licensee or a licensed tobacco dealer.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Section 37 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: If manufactured tobacco or cigars manufactured in Canada are not stamped by a tobacco licensee, the tobacco licensee shall immediately enter the tobacco or cigars into the licensee’s excise warehouse.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Subsections 38(1) to (4) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: No warehousing of tobacco without markings

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (3) and (4), no person shall enter into an excise warehouse a container of manufactured tobacco or cigars unless the container has printed on it or affixed to it tobacco markings and other prescribed information.

Subsection.MarginalNote: No delivery of imported tobacco without markings
Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (3), no person shall deliver a container of imported manufactured tobacco or cigars that does not have printed on it or affixed to it tobacco markings and other prescribed information to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a duty free shop for sale or offer for sale in accordance with the Customs Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an accredited representative; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a customs bonded warehouse.

Subsection.Text: A container of manufactured tobacco does not require tobacco markings to be printed on or affixed to it if the brand of the tobacco is not commonly sold in Canada and is prescribed.

A container of cigarettes does not require tobacco markings to be printed on or affixed to it if the cigarettes are of a particular type or formulation manufactured in Canada and exported under a brand that is also applied to cigarettes of a different type or formulation that are manufactured and sold in Canada and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: cigarettes of the particular type or formulation exported under that brand are prescribed cigarettes; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: cigarettes of the particular type or formulation have never been sold in Canada under that brand or any other brand.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Subsection 41(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister may authorize a tobacco licensee to import any manufactured tobacco or cigars manufactured in Canada by the licensee for re-working or destruction by the licensee in accordance with subsection (1).
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Section 46 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: The duty imposed under section 42 is relieved on raw leaf tobacco that is imported by a tobacco licensee or a licensed tobacco dealer.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Subsection 50(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:
No person shall remove from an excise warehouse or a special excise warehouse manufactured tobacco or cigars manufactured in Canada.
Subsection 50(10) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Removal from warehouse for re-working or destruction
Subsection.Text: Subject to the regulations, manufactured tobacco or cigars manufactured in Canada may be removed from the excise warehouse of the tobacco licensee who manufactured them if they are removed for re-working or destruction by the licensee in accordance with section 41.
Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Subsection 51(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
No person shall remove imported manufactured tobacco or cigars from an excise warehouse.
The portion of subsection 51(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Subject to the regulations, imported manufactured tobacco or cigars may be removed from an excise warehouse
Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Section 52 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: No special excise warehouse licensee shall store manufactured tobacco or cigars that are manufactured in Canada in their special excise warehouse for any purpose other than the sale and distribution of the tobacco or cigars to an accredited representative for the personal or official use of the representative.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 59:

Section.Text: If alcohol is imported under circumstances in which subsection 3(1) of the Importation of Intoxicating Liquors Act applies, the alcohol is deemed, for the purposes of this Act and subsection 21.2(3) of the Customs Tariff, to have been imported by the person who would have been the importer in the absence of that subsection 3(1) and not by Her Majesty in right of a province or a liquor authority.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Subsection 60(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Subsection (1) does not apply to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the packaging of spirits from a marked special container by a purchaser at a bottle-your-own premises; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the production of spirits for the purpose or as a consequence of the analysis of the composition of a substance containing absolute ethyl alcohol.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Section 61 of the Act is amended by striking out the word “or” at the end of paragraph (a), by adding the word “or” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: possesses the still or equipment solely for the purpose of producing spirits for the purpose or as a consequence of the analysis of the composition of a substance containing absolute ethyl alcohol.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 62:

Section.Text: Except as permitted under section 130, no person shall use bulk spirits to fortify bulk wine.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
The portion of section 66 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Sections 67 to 72, 75, 76, 80, 85, 88, 97 to 100 and 102 do not apply to imported alcohol or specially denatured alcohol that is, in accordance with the Customs Act, the Customs Tariff and the regulations made under those Acts,

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Subsection 70(2) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of bulk spirits produced for the purpose or as a consequence of the analysis of the composition of a substance containing absolute ethyl alcohol, to a person who, having produced those spirits, possesses them during the period of analysis;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Paragraph 73(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: use it in accordance with section 130, 131 or 131.1;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Sections 74 and 75 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.Text: A person who possesses bulk spirits produced for the purpose or as a consequence of the analysis of the composition of a substance containing absolute ethyl alcohol shall, immediately after the analysis is complete, destroy or dispose of the spirits in a manner approved by the Minister.

Subsection.Text: No person shall import bulk spirits other than a spirits licensee, a licensed user or, if the spirits are in a special container, an excise warehouse licensee in accordance with section 80.
No person shall import bulk wine other than a wine licensee, a licensed user or, if the wine is in a special container, an excise warehouse licensee in accordance with section 85.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Subsection 88(2) of the Act is amended by striking out the word “and” at the end of paragraph (g) and by adding the following after paragraph (h):

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: that is wine referred to in paragraph 135(2)(b) may be possessed by any person; and

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: that is wine produced or packaged by a wine licensee and removed from the excise warehouse of the licensee and that is to be provided free of charge to individuals as a sample consumed at the premises where the licensee produces or packages wine may be possessed by the licensee or those individuals at those premises.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 93:

Section.Text: A licensed user shall not use or dispose of a restricted formulation other than in accordance with the conditions or restrictions imposed by the Minister under section 143.
No person other than a licensed user or an alcohol registrant shall possess a restricted formulation.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 117:

Section.Text: If bulk spirits are produced from bulk wine, the wine licensee or licensed user who was responsible for the wine before it was used to produce the spirits ceases to be responsible for the wine at the time the spirits are produced.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Section 131 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: A licensed user who is also a spirits licensee may blend bulk wine with spirits if the resulting product is spirits.
A licensed user who is also a spirits licensee may use bulk wine to produce spirits.

Section.MarginalNote: Deemed production of spirits — blending wine

Subsection.Text: If wine is blended with bulk spirits and the resulting product is spirits,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the duty imposed under section 122 or levied under section 21.1 of the Customs Tariff on the spirits that were blended with the wine is relieved; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the resulting spirits are deemed to be produced at the time of the blending.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Deemed production of spirits — other blending
Subsection.Text: If a material or substance, other than spirits or wine, containing absolute ethyl alcohol is blended with bulk spirits or wine and the resulting product is spirits,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the duty imposed under section 122 or levied under section 21.1 of the Customs Tariff on the spirits that were blended with the material or substance is relieved; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the resulting spirits are deemed to be produced at the time of the blending.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Paragraph 135(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: produced by a wine licensee and packaged by or on behalf of the licensee during a fiscal month in a particular fiscal year of the licensee if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the total sales by the licensee of products that are subject to duty under subsection (1), or that would have been so subject to duty in the absence of this subsection, in the fiscal year ending immediately before the particular fiscal year did not exceed $50,000, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the total sales by the licensee of those products during the particular fiscal year before the fiscal month did not exceed $50,000.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Section 136 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Duty payable on removal from warehouse

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), if packaged wine is removed from an excise warehouse for entry into the duty-paid market, duty is payable on the wine at the time of its removal and is payable by the excise warehouse licensee.

If a small wine licensee removes packaged wine that the licensee produced or packaged from the excise warehouse of the licensee for delivery and sale on a consignment basis at a retail store operated on behalf of two or more small wine licensees and that is not located on the premises of a wine licensee, the wine is deemed to be removed from the warehouse for entry into the duty-paid market at the time the wine is sold.

In this section, a wine licensee is a small wine licensee during a fiscal year of the licensee if, in the previous fiscal year, the total amount of wine sold by the licensee did not exceed 60,000 litres.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Subsection 138(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of packaged wine described by subsection 136(2), as being in a store described by that subsection;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Subsection 145(2) of the Act is amended by striking out the word “or” at the end of paragraph (b), by adding the word “or” at the end of paragraph (c) and by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of wine, taken for use by an excise warehouse licensee if that licensee is also the wine licensee who produced or packaged the wine and the wine is provided free of charge to individuals for consumption as a sample at the premises where the licensee produces or packages wine.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Section 147 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

Duty is not payable on non-duty-paid packaged wine, other than wine contained in a marked special container, that is removed from the excise warehouse of the wine licensee who produced or packaged the wine if the wine is to be provided free of charge to individuals as a sample consumed at the premises where the licensee produces or packages wine.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Paragraph 151(2)(a) of the Act is amended by striking out the word “or” at the end of subparagraph (vii) and by replacing subparagraph (viii) with the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of packaged wine described in subsection 136(2), delivery to a store described in that subsection, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ix)
Subparagraph.Text: export;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 151(2) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: non-duty-paid packaged wine, other than wine in a marked special container, if the warehouse is the excise warehouse of the wine licensee who produced or packaged the wine and the wine is to be provided free of charge to individuals as a sample consumed at the premises where the licensee produces or packages wine;

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 153:

Section.Text: If non-duty-paid packaged wine that has been removed from an excise warehouse under subparagraph 151(2)(a)(viii) is returned to that warehouse under prescribed conditions, the wine may, if it had not been entered into the duty-paid market, be entered into the warehouse as non-duty-paid packaged wine.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Subsection 160(1) of the Act is renumbered as section 160 and subsection 160(2) of the Act is repealed.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Section 173 of the Act, as enacted by subsection 116(1) of the Budget Implementation Act, 2006, chapter 4 of the Statutes of Canada, 2006, is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: The Minister may, on or before the particular day that is 10 calendar years after the day an amount was required to be paid by a person under this Act, or on application by the person on or before the particular day, waive or reduce any interest on the amount payable by the person under section 170.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force, or is deemed to have come into force, on April 1, 2007.

Section.Text: Paragraph 177(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount has previously been refunded, remitted or paid to that person, or applied against an amount owed by the person to Her Majesty, under this or any other Act of Parliament; or

Subsection.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 181:

Section.Text: The Minister may refund to a duty free shop licensee the special duty under section 53 that was paid on imported manufactured tobacco that is destroyed by the licensee in accordance with the Customs Act if the licensee applies for the refund within two years after the tobacco is destroyed.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Subsection 188(6) of the Act, as enacted by subsection 117(5) of the Budget Implementation Act, 2006, chapter 4 of the Statutes of Canada, 2006, is replaced by the following:

An overpayment of duty payable for a fiscal month of a person and interest on the overpayment shall not be applied under paragraph (4)(b) or refunded under paragraph (4)(c) unless the person has, before the day on which notice of the assessment is sent to the person, filed all returns and other records of which the Minister has knowledge and that the person was required to file with

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister under this Act, the Air Travellers Security Charge Act, the Excise Act, the Excise Tax Act and the Income Tax Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Public Safety and Emergency Preparedness under the Customs Act.

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 188(7)(b)(ii) of the Act, as enacted by subsection 117(6) of the Budget Implementation Act, 2006, chapter 4 of the Statutes of Canada, 2006, is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the person has, before the day on which notice of the assessment is sent to the person, filed all returns and other records of which the Minister has knowledge and that the person was required to file with

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the Minister under this Act, the Air Travellers Security Charge Act, the Excise Act, the Excise Tax Act and the Income Tax Act, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the Minister of Public Safety and Emergency Preparedness under the Customs Act.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) come into force, or are deemed to have come into force, on April 1, 2007.
Subsection 189(4) of the Act, as enacted by subsection 118(1) of the Budget Implementation Act, 2006, chapter 4 of the Statutes of Canada, 2006, is replaced by the following:

A refund shall not be paid until the person has filed with the Minister or the Minister of Public Safety and Emergency Preparedness all returns and other records of which the Minister has knowledge and that are required to be filed under this Act, the Air Travellers Security Charge Act, the Customs Act, the Excise Act, the Excise Tax Act and the Income Tax Act.
Subsection (1) comes into force, or is deemed to have come into force, on April 1, 2007.
Subsection 196(4) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:
Le ministre peut recevoir la demande qui n’a pas été faite en conformité avec le paragraphe (3).
Subparagraph 196(7)(b)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: within the time limited under this Act for objecting, the person

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: was unable to act or to give a mandate to act in their name, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: had a bona fide intention to object to the assessment,

Section.Text: Subparagraph 197(6)(b)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: within the time limited under this Act for objecting, the person

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: was unable to act or to give a mandate to act in their name, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: had a bona fide intention to object to the assessment,

Section.Text: Subparagraph 199(5)(b)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: within the time limited under section 198 for appealing, the person

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: was unable to act or to give a mandate to act in their name, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: had a bona fide intention to appeal,

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 208(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Requirement to provide records or information

Subsection.Text: Despite any other provision of this Act, the Minister may, subject to subsection (2), for any purpose related to the administration or enforcement of a listed international agreement or of this Act, by notice served personally or by registered or certified mail, require any person to provide the Minister, within any reasonable time that is stipulated in the notice, with
Subparagraph 211(6)(e)(v) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: to an official of a department or agency of the Government of Canada or of a province as to the name, address, telephone number, occupation, size or type of business of a person, solely for the purpose of enabling the department or agency to obtain statistical data for research and analysis,

Subsection.Text: Subsection 211(6) of the Act is amended by striking out the word “or” at the end of paragraph (j) and by adding the following after paragraph (k):

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: provide confidential information, or allow the inspection of or access to confidential information, as the case may be, solely for the purposes of a provision contained in a listed international agreement; or

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: provide confidential information to any person solely for the purpose of enabling the Chief Statistician, within the meaning assigned by section 2 of the Statistics Act, to provide to a statistical agency of a province data concerning business activities carried on in the province, if the information is used by the statistical agency solely for research and analysis and the statistical agency is authorized under the law of the province to collect the same or similar information on its own behalf in respect of such activities.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 217(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Every person who contravenes section 63 or 73, subsection 78(1) or 83(1) or section 90, 93.1, 93.2 or 96 is guilty of an offence and liable
Subparagraph 217(2)(a)(iii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: $10 multiplied by the number of litres of specially denatured alcohol or a restricted formulation to which the offence relates, and

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 217(3)(a)(iii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: $20 multiplied by the number of litres of specially denatured alcohol or a restricted formulation to which the offence relates, and

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 218(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Punishment for more serious alcohol offences

Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes any of sections 67, 69 to 72, 75 or 88 or subsection 101(1) or (2) is guilty of an offence and liable

Section.Text: Paragraph 221(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to whom confidential information has been provided for a purpose pursuant to paragraph 211(6)(b), (d), (h), (l) or (m), or

Section.Text: Section 234 of the Act is replaced by the following:
Every person who contravenes section 38, 40, 41, 49, 61, 62.1, 99, 149 or 151 is liable to a penalty of not more than $25,000.
Subsection 236(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every tobacco licensee or customs bonded warehouse licensee is liable to a penalty if manufactured tobacco on which duty was imposed under section 42 at a rate set out in paragraph 1(a), 2(a) or 3(a) of Schedule 1 is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a tobacco licensee,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: delivered by the licensee other than to a duty free shop or customs bonded warehouse or to a person for use as ships’ stores in accordance with the Ships’ Stores Regulations, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: exported by the licensee other than for delivery to a foreign duty free shop or as foreign ships’ stores; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a customs bonded warehouse licensee, delivered by the licensee other than to a person for use as ships’ stores in accordance with the Ships’ Stores Regulations.

Section.Text: Subsection 237(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every excise warehouse licensee is liable to a penalty equal to 200% of the duty imposed on packaged alcohol that was removed from the warehouse of the licensee for a purpose described in section 147 if the alcohol is not delivered, exported or provided, as the case may be, for that purpose.

Section.Text: Section 243 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Unless section 239, 241, 242 or 243.1 or subsection (2) applies, every person who contravenes section 73, 74 or 90 is liable to a penalty equal to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the contravention relates to spirits, 200% of the duty that was imposed on the spirits; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the contravention relates to wine, $1.24 per litre of that wine.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Contravention of section 73 or 90 by licensed user
Subsection.Text: Every licensed user who exports, gives possession of or takes for use alcohol in contravention of section 73 or 90 is liable to a penalty equal to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the contravention relates to spirits, the duty that was imposed on the spirits; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the contravention relates to wine, $0.62 per litre of that wine.

Section.Text: Every person who contravenes section 76, 89 or 91 is liable to a penalty equal to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the contravention relates to spirits, the duty that was imposed on the spirits; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the contravention relates to wine, $0.62 per litre of that wine.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 247:

Section.MarginalNote: Unauthorized possession, etc., of restricted formulation
Section.Text: Every person who contravenes section 93.1 or 93.2 is liable to a penalty of $10 per litre of restricted formulation to which the contravention relates.

Subsection.Text: Section 255.1 of the Act, as enacted by subsection 121(1) of the Budget Implementation Act, 2006, chapter 4 of the Statutes of Canada, 2006, is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Waiving or reducing failure to file penalty
Section.Text: The Minister may, on or before the day that is 10 calendar years after the end of a fiscal month of a person, or on application by the person on or before that day, waive or reduce any penalty payable by the person under section 251.1 in respect of a return for the fiscal month.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force, or is deemed to have come into force, on April 1, 2007.
Section 264 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Certain things not to be returned
Section.Text: Despite this Act, any alcohol, specially denatured alcohol, restricted formulation, raw leaf tobacco or tobacco product that is seized under section 260 must not be returned to the person from whom it was seized or any other person unless it was seized in error.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.

Subsection 266(2) of the Act is amended by striking out the word “and” at the end of paragraph (b), by adding the word “and” at the end of paragraph (c) and by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a seized restricted formulation only to a licensed user.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.

Section.Text: The definition common-law partner in subsection 297(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: common-law partner of an individual at any time means a person who is the common-law partner of the individual at that time for the purposes of the Income Tax Act.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 304(1)(n) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the sale under section 266 of alcohol, tobacco products, raw leaf tobacco, specially denatured alcohol or restricted formulations seized under section 260;

Subsection.Text: Section 304 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

For greater certainty, a regulation made under this Act may incorporate by reference any material, regardless of its source and either as it exists on a particular date or as amended from time to time.
Subsection (2) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 315:

If, during the period beginning on July 1, 2003 and ending on July 1, 2009, sections 7, 8, 9, 12 and 15 of the Distillery Regulations, C.R.C., c. 569, would have applied in any circumstance had those sections, as they read on June 30, 2003, been in force and section 1.1 of the Excise Act not been enacted, those sections apply, with any modifications that the circumstances require.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Transitional application of Distillery Departmental Regulations
Subsection.Text: If, during the period beginning on July 1, 2003 and ending on July 1, 2009, sections 13 and 14 of the Distillery Departmental Regulations, C.R.C., c. 570, would have applied in any circumstance had those sections, as they read on June 30, 2003, been in force and section 1.1 of the Excise Act not been enacted, those sections apply, with any modifications that the circumstances require.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 316:

Section.MarginalNote: Refund for re-worked or destroyed tobacco product
Section.Text: If duty imposed under the Excise Act and tax imposed under section 23 of the Excise Tax Act on a tobacco product manufactured in Canada had become payable before the implementation date and a tobacco licensee under this Act who was, before that day, licensed under those Acts to manufacture the product re-works or destroys, on or after that day, the product in a manner authorized by the Minister, section 181 applies as though that duty and tax were duty paid under this Act.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 317:

Section.MarginalNote: Imported tobacco delivered to duty free shop before implementation date
Section.Text: If, on the implementation date, imported manufactured tobacco on which tax under section 23.12 of the Excise Tax Act was paid is possessed by a duty free shop licensee and no application for a refund of the tax has been made under that Act, this Act applies in respect of the tobacco as though the tax were special duty under section 53.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Schedule II to the Access to Information Act is amended by adding, in alphabetical order, a reference to
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Subsection 2(1) of the Customs Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: licensed user has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001;
restricted formulation has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Subsection 97.25(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to the regulations, the Minister, on giving 30 days’ notice in writing to the debtor at the debtor’s latest known address, may direct that any good imported or reported for exportation by or on behalf of the debtor, or any conveyance, that has been detained be sold

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the good is spirits or specially denatured alcohol, to a spirits licensee;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the good is wine, to a wine licensee;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the good is raw leaf tobacco or a tobacco product, to a tobacco licensee;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if the good is a restricted formulation, to a licensed user; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, by public auction or public tender or by the Minister of Public Works and Government Services under the Surplus Crown Assets Act.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Subsection 117(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Despite subsection (1), if spirits, wine, specially denatured alcohol, restricted formulations, raw leaf tobacco or tobacco products are seized under this Act, they shall not be returned to the person from whom they were seized or any other person unless they were seized in error.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.

Subsection 119.1(1.1) of the Act is amended by striking out the word “and” at the end of paragraph (b), by adding the word “and” at the end of paragraph (c) and by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a restricted formulation may only be to a licensed user.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
The portion of subsection 142(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Disposal of things abandoned or forfeit

Subsection.Text: Unless the thing is spirits, specially denatured alcohol, a restricted formulation, wine, raw leaf tobacco or a tobacco product, anything that has been abandoned to Her Majesty in right of Canada under this Act and anything the forfeiture of which is final under this Act shall
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Section 142.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Dealing with abandoned or forfeited alcohol, etc.

Subsection.Text: If spirits, specially denatured alcohol, a restricted formulation, wine, raw leaf tobacco or a tobacco product is abandoned or finally forfeited under this Act, the Minister may sell, destroy or otherwise deal with it.
Subject to the regulations, the sale under subsection (1) of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: spirits or specially denatured alcohol may only be to a spirits licensee;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: wine may only be to a wine licensee;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: raw leaf tobacco or a tobacco product may only be to a tobacco licensee; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a restricted formulation may only be to a licensed user.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.

Section.Text: Subsection 164(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (h.1):

Paragraph.Label: (h.2)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the sale of alcohol, a tobacco product, raw leaf tobacco, specially denatured alcohol or a restricted formulation detained, seized, abandoned or forfeited under this Act;

Subsection.Text: The definitions “beer or malt liquor” and wine in section 21 of the Customs Tariff are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: beer or malt liquor means beer or malt liquor, within the meaning of section 4 of the Excise Act, of tariff item No. 2202.90.10 or heading 22.03, that is classified under that heading or tariff item or with the container in which it is imported.

wine means wine, as defined in section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001, of heading 22.04, 22.05 or 22.06, other than of tariff item No. 2204.10.90, 2204.21.32, 2204.21.49, 2204.29.32, 2204.29.49, 2204.30.90, 2205.10.30, 2205.90.30, 2206.00.19, 2206.00.22, 2206.00.39, 2206.00.49, 2206.00.72 or 2206.00.93, that is classified under that heading or with the container in which it is imported.

Subsection.Text: Section 21 of the French version of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: utilisateur agréé S’entend au sens de l’article 2 de la Loi de 2001 sur l’accise.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (2) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.
Paragraph 215(1)(b) of the Excise Tax Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of all duties and taxes, if any, payable on the goods under the Customs Tariff, the Excise Act, 2001, the Special Import Measures Act, this Act (other than this Part) or any other law relating to customs.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2003.

Heading.Label: PART 3
Heading.TitleText: AMENDMENTS TO THE AIR TRAVELLERS SECURITY CHARGE ACT

Subsection.Text: The definition listed airport in section 2 of the Air Travellers Security Charge Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: listed airport means an airport listed in the schedule.

Subsection.Text: Section 2 of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: registered charity has the same meaning as in subsection 248(1) of the Income Tax Act.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (2) is deemed to have come into force on April 1, 2002.
Section 11 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):
No charge is payable in respect of an air transportation service that is acquired

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: by a person for the purpose of re-selling the service, if the person sells the service to another person before April 1, 2002 and makes full and final payment to the air carrier in respect of the service before May 1, 2002; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: by a registered charity from an air carrier for no consideration, if the service is donated by the charity to an individual for no consideration and in pursuit of its charitable purposes.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on April 1, 2002.
Subsection 30(1) of the Act, as enacted by subsection 102(1) of the Budget Implementation Act, 2006, chapter 4 of the Statutes of Canada, 2006, is replaced by the following:

The Minister may, on or before the day that is 10 calendar years after the end of a fiscal month of a person, or on application by the person on or before that day, waive or reduce any interest payable by the person under this Act on an amount that is required to be paid by the person under this Act in respect of the fiscal month.
Subsection (1) comes into force, or is deemed to have come into force, on April 1, 2007.
Subsection 44(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:
An application must be made by delivering or mailing, to the Chief of Appeals in a Tax Services Office of the Agency, the application accompanied by a copy of the notice of objection.
Subsection 44(4) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:
Le ministre peut recevoir la demande qui n’a pas été faite en conformité avec le paragraphe (3).
Subparagraph 44(7)(b)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: within the time limited under this Act for objecting, the person

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: was unable to act or to give a mandate to act in their name, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: had a bona fide intention to object to the assessment,

Section.Text: Subparagraph 45(6)(b)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: within the time limited under this Act for objecting, the person

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: was unable to act or to give a mandate to act in their name, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: had a bona fide intention to object to the assessment,

Section.Text: Subparagraph 47(5)(b)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: within the time limited under section 46 for appealing, the person

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: was unable to act or to give a mandate to act in their name, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: had a bona fide intention to appeal,

Subsection.Text: Subsection 52(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a question set out in an application is determined by the Tax Court, the Minister or any of the persons who have been served with a copy of the application and who are named in an order of the Court under subsection (4) may, in accordance with the provisions of this Act, the Tax Court of Canada Act or the Federal Courts Act, as they relate to appeals from or applications for judicial review of decisions of the Tax Court, appeal from the determination.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on July 2, 2003.
Subsection 55(1) of the Act, as enacted by subsection 107(1) of the Budget Implementation Act, 2006, chapter 4 of the Statutes of Canada, 2006, is replaced by the following:

The Minister may, on or before the day that is 10 calendar years after the end of a fiscal month of a person, or on application by the person on or before that day, waive or cancel any penalty payable by the person under section 53 in respect of the fiscal month.
Subsection (1) comes into force, or is deemed to have come into force, on April 1, 2007.

Section.Text: Section 84 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may, by regulation, amend the schedule by adding, deleting or varying the reference to an airport.

Section.Text: The schedule to the Act is amended by replacing the reference “(Section 2)” after the heading “SCHEDULE” with the reference “(Section 2 and subsection 84(1.1))”.

Subsection.Text: The schedule to the Act is amended by striking out the following under the heading “Quebec”:

Provision.Text: La Grande-3
La Grande-4

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on December 23, 2004.

Section.Text: The schedule to the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order under the heading “Quebec”:

Provision.Text: Rivière-Rouge (Mont Tremblant International)

Section.Text: The schedule to the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order under the heading “Alberta”:

Provision.Text: Red Deer Regional

Subsection.Text: If Bill C-28, introduced in the 1st session of the 39th Parliament and entitled the Budget Implementation Act, 2006, No. 2, receives royal assent and section 57 of that Act comes into force, then paragraph 88(2)(i) of the Excise Act, 2001, as enacted by subsection 96(1) of this Act, is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: that is wine referred to in paragraph 135(2)(a) or (b) may be possessed by any person; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to wine packaged after June 2006.
